---
title: "hrcbd"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
# Load packages used for data manipulation, modeling output tidying, and plotting.
library(tidyverse)
library(broom)

# Simulate a randomized complete block design with constant within-cell variance.
# The treatment effects are set to zero so rejection rates estimate Type I error.
R2_sim_het <- function(blocks,
                   treatments,
                   mu,
                   sigma_b,
                   R2_target,
                   response_type = "normal") {

  # Create one observation for every treatment-by-block combination.
  data <- expand.grid(
    block = factor(1:blocks),
    treatment = factor(1:treatments)
  )

  # Set all treatment effects to zero under the null hypothesis.
  alpha_t <- rep(0, treatments)
  
  # Generate random block effects with user-specified variability.
  beta_b <- rnorm(blocks, 0, sigma_b)

  # Compute the block-effect sum of squares contribution used to calibrate R^2.
  S <- treatments * sum((beta_b - mean(beta_b))^2)

  # Use a constant variance function for the homoscedastic simulation setting.
  Vbar <- 1

  # Store degrees-of-freedom-related constants used in the R^2 calibration.
  A1 <- treatments + blocks - 2
  B1 <- treatments * blocks - 1

  # Choose the residual scale so the expected linear-model R^2 targets R2_target.
  sigma <- sqrt(S * (1 - R2_target) /(Vbar * (R2_target * B1 - A1)))

  # Compute the treatment-by-block mean for each simulated observation.
  mu_tb <- mu + alpha_t[data$treatment] + beta_b[data$block]

  # Assign the cell-specific variance multiplier.
  V_tb <- 1

  # Generate normal random errors using the calibrated residual scale.
  e <- rnorm(nrow(data), mean = 0, sd = sigma * sqrt(V_tb))

  # Construct the normal response.
  y1 <- mu_tb + e

  # Compute the cell-specific variance on the normal scale.
  Var_tb <- sigma^2 * V_tb

  # Compute transformation constants so the lognormal response preserves target moments.
  a_tb <- sqrt(log(1 + Var_tb / mu_tb^2)) / sqrt(Var_tb)

  # Compute the location constant for the moment-matched lognormal transformation.
  b_tb <- log(mu_tb) - a_tb * mu_tb - (a_tb^2 * Var_tb) / 2

  # Select the requested response scale for the same simulated data.
  if (response_type == "normal") {
  response <- y1
  } else if (response_type == "lognormal") {
  response <- exp(a_tb * y1 + b_tb)
  } else if (response_type == "log") {
  response <- a_tb * y1 + b_tb
  } else if (response_type == "exp") {
  response <- exp(y1)
  }

  # Attach the simulated response to the design data frame.
  data$response <- response

  # Fit the additive randomized complete block ANOVA model.
  model <- aov(response ~ treatment + block, data)
  anova_tbl <- anova(model) |> tidy()

  # Extract the treatment row from the ANOVA table.
  treatment_row <- anova_tbl |>
    filter(term == "treatment")

  # Save the treatment F statistic and corresponding p-value.
  F_stat <- treatment_row$statistic
  p_value <- treatment_row$p.value

  # Record whether the treatment test rejects at alpha = 0.05.
  reject_F <- p_value < 0.05

  # Record whether any Tukey-adjusted pairwise treatment comparison rejects.
  tukey_reject <- TukeyHSD(model, "treatment")$treatment |>
    as.data.frame() |>
    pull(`p adj`) |>
    (\(x) any(x < 0.05))()

  # Compute the observed model R^2 for this simulation replicate.
  R2 <- summary(lm(response ~ treatment + block, data))$r.squared

  # Extract the residual mean square.
  MS_error <- anova_tbl |>
    filter(term == "Residuals") |>
    pull(meansq)
  
  # Extract the treatment mean square.
  MS_treatment <- anova_tbl |>
  filter(term == "treatment") |>
  pull(meansq)

  # Return the replicate-level operating characteristics and ANOVA components.
  tibble(
    F_stat = F_stat,
    reject_F = reject_F,
    R2 = R2,
    tukey_reject = tukey_reject,
    MSE = MS_error,
    MSa = MS_treatment
  )
}
```

```{r}
# Set the random seed so the simulation study is reproducible.
set.seed(100)

# Specify the target proportion of variation explained by treatment and block terms.
R2_target <- 0.5

# Run 10,000 homoscedastic simulations and analyze the untransformed response.
results_normal <- map_dfr(1:10000, ~ R2_sim_het(7, 8, 20, 3, R2_target, "normal"))

# Run 10,000 homoscedastic simulations and analyze the moment-matched lognormal response.
results_lognormal <- map_dfr(1:10000, ~ R2_sim_het(7, 8, 20, 3, R2_target, "lognormal"))

# Run 10,000 homoscedastic simulations and analyze the log-scale transformed response.
results_log <- map_dfr(1:10000, ~ R2_sim_het(7, 8, 20, 3, R2_target, "log"))

# Run 10,000 homoscedastic simulations and analyze the exponentiated response.
results_exp <- map_dfr(1:10000, ~ R2_sim_het(7, 8, 20, 3, R2_target, "exp"))

# Combine simulation outputs and summarize rejection rates and ANOVA diagnostics by response scale.
results <- bind_rows(
  normal = results_normal,
  lognormal = results_lognormal,
  log = results_log,
  exp = results_exp,
  .id = "dataset"
) |>
  group_by(dataset) |>
  summarise(
    type1_error = mean(reject_F),
    mean_F = mean(F_stat),
    var_F = var(F_stat),
    mean_MSa = mean(MSa),
    var_MSa = var(MSa),
    mean_MSE = mean(MSE),
    var_MSE = var(MSE),
    mean_R2 = mean(R2),
    tukey_fwer = mean(tukey_reject)
  )
```

```{r}
# Plot the Type I error rate for each response transformation.
ggplot(results, aes(x = dataset, y = type1_error)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "red3") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.05, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    x = "Transformation",
    y = "Type I Error Rate",
    title = "Type I Error Rate for Homoscedastic Data With Transformations"
  ) +
  ylim(0, 0.08) +
  theme_bw()
```

```{r}
# Simulate a randomized complete block design with variance proportional to the squared mean.
# Treatment effects remain zero so rejection rates estimate null Type I error.
R2_sim_mu2 <- function(blocks,
                   treatments,
                   mu,
                   sigma_b,
                   R2_target,
                   response_type = "normal") {

  # Create one observation for every treatment-by-block combination.
  data <- expand.grid(
    block = factor(1:blocks),
    treatment = factor(1:treatments)
  )

  # Set all treatment effects to zero under the null hypothesis.
  alpha_t <- rep(0, treatments)
  
  # Generate random block effects with user-specified variability.
  beta_b <- rnorm(blocks, 0, sigma_b)

  # Compute the block-effect sum of squares contribution used to calibrate R^2.
  S <- treatments * sum((beta_b - mean(beta_b))^2)

  # Build the matrix of treatment-by-block means used by the variance function.
  mean_matrix <- outer(
    alpha_t,
    beta_b,
    function(alpha, beta) mu + alpha + beta
  )

  # Set the variance multiplier proportional to the squared mean.
  variance_matrix <- mean_matrix^2

  # Average the variance multipliers for the R^2 calibration.
  Vbar <- mean(variance_matrix)

  # Store degrees-of-freedom-related constants used in the R^2 calibration.
  A1 <- treatments + blocks - 2
  B1 <- treatments * blocks - 1

  # Choose the residual scale so the expected linear-model R^2 targets R2_target.
  sigma <- sqrt(S * (1 - R2_target) /(Vbar * (R2_target * B1 - A1)))

  # Compute the treatment-by-block mean for each simulated observation.
  mu_tb <- mu + alpha_t[data$treatment] + beta_b[data$block]

  # Assign the observation-specific variance multiplier.
  V_tb <- mu_tb^2

  # Generate normal random errors using the calibrated heteroscedastic scale.
  e <- rnorm(nrow(data), mean = 0, sd = sigma * sqrt(V_tb))

  # Construct the normal response.
  y1 <- mu_tb + e

  # Compute the cell-specific variance on the normal scale.
  Var_tb <- sigma^2 * V_tb

  # Compute transformation constants so the lognormal response preserves target moments.
  a_tb <- sqrt(log(1 + Var_tb / mu_tb^2)) / sqrt(Var_tb)

  # Compute the location constant for the moment-matched lognormal transformation.
  b_tb <- log(mu_tb) - a_tb * mu_tb - (a_tb^2 * Var_tb) / 2

  # Select the requested response scale for the same simulated data.
  if (response_type == "normal") {
  response <- y1
  } else if (response_type == "lognormal") {
  response <- exp(a_tb * y1 + b_tb)
  } else if (response_type == "log") {
  response <- a_tb * y1 + b_tb
  } else if (response_type == "exp") {
  response <- exp(y1)
  }

  # Attach the simulated response to the design data frame.
  data$response <- response

  # Fit the additive randomized complete block ANOVA model.
  model <- aov(response ~ treatment + block, data)
  anova_tbl <- anova(model) |> tidy()

  # Extract the treatment row from the ANOVA table.
  treatment_row <- anova_tbl |>
    filter(term == "treatment")

  # Save the treatment F statistic and corresponding p-value.
  F_stat <- treatment_row$statistic
  p_value <- treatment_row$p.value

  # Record whether the treatment test rejects at alpha = 0.05.
  reject_F <- p_value < 0.05

  # Record whether any Tukey-adjusted pairwise treatment comparison rejects.
  tukey_reject <- TukeyHSD(model, "treatment")$treatment |>
    as.data.frame() |>
    pull(`p adj`) |>
    (\(x) any(x < 0.05))()

  # Compute the observed model R^2 for this simulation replicate.
  R2 <- summary(lm(response ~ treatment + block, data))$r.squared

  # Extract the residual mean square.
  MS_error <- anova_tbl |>
    filter(term == "Residuals") |>
    pull(meansq)
  
  # Extract the treatment mean square.
  MS_treatment <- anova_tbl |>
  filter(term == "treatment") |>
  pull(meansq)

  # Return the replicate-level operating characteristics and ANOVA components.
  tibble(
    F_stat = F_stat,
    reject_F = reject_F,
    R2 = R2,
    tukey_reject = tukey_reject,
    MSE = MS_error,
    MSa = MS_treatment
  )
}
```

```{r}
# Reset the random seed so this scenario is reproducible.
set.seed(100)

# Run 10,000 simulations with variance proportional to the squared mean.
results_normal1 <- map_dfr(1:10000, ~ R2_sim_mu2(7, 8, 20, 3, R2_target, "normal"))

# Analyze the lognormal response for the same variance structure.
results_lognormal1 <- map_dfr(1:10000, ~ R2_sim_mu2(7, 8, 20, 3, R2_target, "lognormal"))

# Analyze the log-scale response for the same variance structure.
results_log1 <- map_dfr(1:10000, ~ R2_sim_mu2(7, 8, 20, 3, R2_target, "log"))

# Analyze the exponentiated response for the same variance structure.
results_exp1 <- map_dfr(1:10000, ~ R2_sim_mu2(7, 8, 20, 3, R2_target, "exp"))

# Combine simulation outputs and summarize rejection rates and ANOVA diagnostics by response scale.
results1 <- bind_rows(
  normal = results_normal1,
  lognormal = results_lognormal1,
  log = results_log1,
  exp = results_exp1,
  .id = "dataset"
) |>
  group_by(dataset) |>
  summarise(
    type1_error = mean(reject_F),
    mean_F = mean(F_stat),
    var_F = var(F_stat),
    mean_MSa = mean(MSa),
    var_MSa = var(MSa),
    mean_MSE = mean(MSE),
    var_MSE = var(MSE),
    mean_R2 = mean(R2),
    tukey_fwer = mean(tukey_reject)
  )
```

```{r}
# Plot the Type I error rate when variance is proportional to the squared mean.
ggplot(results1, aes(x = dataset, y = type1_error)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "red3") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.05, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    x = "Transformation",
    y = "Type I Error Rate",
    title = "Type I Error Rate for Normal Data With Transformations",
    subtitle = "Variance proportional to mean"
  ) +
  ylim(0, 0.08) +
  theme_bw()
```

```{r}
# Simulate a randomized complete block design with variance proportional to squared block effects.
# Treatment effects remain zero so rejection rates estimate null Type I error.
R2_sim_b2 <- function(blocks,
                   treatments,
                   mu,
                   sigma_b,
                   R2_target,
                   response_type = "normal") {

  # Create one observation for every treatment-by-block combination.
  data <- expand.grid(
    block = factor(1:blocks),
    treatment = factor(1:treatments)
  )

  # Set all treatment effects to zero under the null hypothesis.
  alpha_t <- rep(0, treatments)
  
  # Generate random block effects with user-specified variability.
  beta_b <- rnorm(blocks, 0, sigma_b)

  # Compute the block-effect sum of squares contribution used to calibrate R^2.
  S <- treatments * sum((beta_b - mean(beta_b))^2)

  # Build the matrix of variance multipliers based on squared block effects.
  variance_matrix <- outer(
    alpha_t,
    beta_b,
    function(alpha, beta) beta^2
  )

  # Average the variance multipliers for the R^2 calibration.
  Vbar <- mean(variance_matrix)

  # Store degrees-of-freedom-related constants used in the R^2 calibration.
  A1 <- treatments + blocks - 2
  B1 <- treatments * blocks - 1

  # Choose the residual scale so the expected linear-model R^2 targets R2_target.
  sigma <- sqrt(S * (1 - R2_target) /(Vbar * (R2_target * B1 - A1)))

  # Compute the treatment-by-block mean for each simulated observation.
  mu_tb <- mu + alpha_t[data$treatment] + beta_b[data$block]

  # Assign the observation-specific variance multiplier from the block effect.
  V_tb <- beta_b[data$block]^2      # variance proportional to beta^2

  # Generate normal random errors using the calibrated heteroscedastic scale.
  e <- rnorm(nrow(data), mean = 0, sd = sigma * sqrt(V_tb))

  # Construct the normal response.
  y1 <- mu_tb + e

  # Compute the cell-specific variance on the normal scale.
  Var_tb <- sigma^2 * V_tb

  # Compute transformation constants so the lognormal response preserves target moments.
  a_tb <- sqrt(log(1 + Var_tb / mu_tb^2)) / sqrt(Var_tb)

  # Compute the location constant for the moment-matched lognormal transformation.
  b_tb <- log(mu_tb) - a_tb * mu_tb - (a_tb^2 * Var_tb) / 2

  # Select the requested response scale for the same simulated data.
  if (response_type == "normal") {
  response <- y1
  } else if (response_type == "lognormal") {
  response <- exp(a_tb * y1 + b_tb)
  } else if (response_type == "log") {
  response <- a_tb * y1 + b_tb
  } else if (response_type == "exp") {
  response <- exp(y1)
  }

  # Attach the simulated response to the design data frame.
  data$response <- response

  # Fit the additive randomized complete block ANOVA model.
  model <- aov(response ~ treatment + block, data)
  anova_tbl <- anova(model) |> tidy()

  # Extract the treatment row from the ANOVA table.
  treatment_row <- anova_tbl |>
    filter(term == "treatment")

  # Save the treatment F statistic and corresponding p-value.
  F_stat <- treatment_row$statistic
  p_value <- treatment_row$p.value

  # Record whether the treatment test rejects at alpha = 0.05.
  reject_F <- p_value < 0.05

  # Record whether any Tukey-adjusted pairwise treatment comparison rejects.
  tukey_reject <- TukeyHSD(model, "treatment")$treatment |>
    as.data.frame() |>
    pull(`p adj`) |>
    (\(x) any(x < 0.05))()

  # Compute the observed model R^2 for this simulation replicate.
  R2 <- summary(lm(response ~ treatment + block, data))$r.squared

  # Extract the residual mean square.
  MS_error <- anova_tbl |>
    filter(term == "Residuals") |>
    pull(meansq)

  # Extract the treatment mean square.
  MS_treatment <- anova_tbl |>
  filter(term == "treatment") |>
  pull(meansq)

  # Return the replicate-level operating characteristics and ANOVA components.
  tibble(
    F_stat = F_stat,
    reject_F = reject_F,
    R2 = R2,
    tukey_reject = tukey_reject,
    MSE = MS_error,
    MSa = MS_treatment
  )
}
```

```{r}
# Reset the random seed so this scenario is reproducible.
set.seed(100)

# Run 10,000 simulations with variance proportional to squared block effects.
results_normal2 <- map_dfr(1:10000, ~ R2_sim_b2(7, 8, 20, 3, R2_target, "normal"))

# Analyze the lognormal response for the block-variance setting.
results_lognormal2 <- map_dfr(1:10000, ~ R2_sim_b2(7, 8, 20, 3, R2_target, "lognormal"))

# Analyze the log-scale response for the block-variance setting.
results_log2 <- map_dfr(1:10000, ~ R2_sim_b2(7, 8, 20, 3, R2_target, "log"))

# Analyze the exponentiated response for the block-variance setting.
results_exp2 <- map_dfr(1:10000, ~ R2_sim_b2(7, 8, 20, 3, R2_target, "exp"))

# Combine simulation outputs and summarize rejection rates and ANOVA diagnostics by response scale.
results2 <- bind_rows(
  normal = results_normal2,
  lognormal = results_lognormal2,
  log = results_log2,
  exp = results_exp2,
  .id = "dataset"
) |>
  group_by(dataset) |>
  summarise(
    type1_error = mean(reject_F),
    mean_F = mean(F_stat),
    var_F = var(F_stat),
    mean_MSa = mean(MSa),
    var_MSa = var(MSa),
    mean_MSE = mean(MSE),
    var_MSE = var(MSE),
    mean_R2 = mean(R2),
    tukey_fwer = mean(tukey_reject)
  )
```

```{r}
# Plot the Type I error rate when variance is proportional to block effects.
ggplot(results2, aes(x = dataset, y = type1_error)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "red3") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.05, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    x = "Transformation",
    y = "Type I Error Rate",
    title = "Type I Error Rate for Heteroscedastic Data With Transformations",
    subtitle = "Variance proportional to block effect"
  ) +
  ylim(0, 0.08) +
  theme_bw()
```

```{r, eval=FALSE}
# Export the summarized simulation results for manuscript tables or external plotting.
write_csv(results, "homo_data.csv")
write_csv(results1, "hetero_mean_data.csv")
write_csv(results2, "hetero_beta_data.csv")
```

```{r}
# Simulate representative response distributions for exploratory histogram displays.
sim_hist <- function(blocks,
                   treatments,
                   mu,
                   sigma_b,
                   R2_target) {

  # Create one observation for every treatment-by-block combination.
  data <- expand.grid(
    block = factor(1:blocks),
    treatment = factor(1:treatments)
  )

  # Set all treatment effects to zero for the illustrative distribution simulation.
  alpha_t <- rep(0, treatments)
  
  # Generate random block effects.
  beta_b <- rnorm(blocks, 0, sigma_b)

  # Compute the block-effect sum of squares contribution used to calibrate R^2.
  S <- treatments * sum((beta_b - mean(beta_b))^2)

  # Use a constant variance multiplier for the illustrative normal response.
  Vbar <- 1

  # Store degrees-of-freedom-related constants used in the R^2 calibration.
  A1 <- treatments + blocks - 2
  B1 <- treatments * blocks - 1

  # Choose the residual scale so the expected linear-model R^2 targets R2_target.
  sigma <- sqrt(S * (1 - R2_target) /(Vbar * (R2_target * B1 - A1)))

  # Compute the treatment-by-block mean for each simulated observation.
  mu_tb <- mu + alpha_t[data$treatment] + beta_b[data$block]

  # Generate a normally distributed response centered at the treatment-by-block means.
  normal <- mu_tb + rnorm(nrow(data), 0, sigma)

  # Generate a lognormal response for shape comparison.
  lognormal <- rlnorm(
    nrow(data),
    meanlog = 3,
    sdlog = 0.5
  )

  # Generate an exponential response with mean 20 for shape comparison.
  exponential <- rexp(
    nrow(data),
    rate = 1 / 20)

  # Log-transform the lognormal response to visualize the induced log-scale distribution.
  log_dist <- lognormal |> log()
  
  # Return all illustrative response distributions in a single data frame.
  tibble(
    normal = normal,
    lognormal = lognormal,
    log = log_dist,
    exp = exponential
    )
}
```

```{r}
# Generate data used for the illustrative histograms.
normal_data <- sim_hist(20, 20, 20, 10, 0.5)

# Store each simulated distribution as a separate vector for plotting.
normal_hist <- normal_data$normal
lognormal_hist <- normal_data$lognormal
log_hist <- normal_data$log
exp_hist <- normal_data$exp

# Plot histograms for the four illustrative response distributions.
hist(normal_hist, breaks = 50)
hist(lognormal_hist, breaks = 50)
hist(log_hist, breaks = 50)
hist(exp_hist, breaks = 50)
```
